source_jobs.title: Growth Strategist (Chicago)
source_jobs.description:
About the Company

Why We Built Lyric: Supply chains are more critical and complex than ever. Every day, large enterprises navigate trillions of possible decisions that could impact the bottom line. Powerful algorithms and AI can address these problems, yet most organizations struggle to leverage supply chain AI at scale. The current SCM technologies are either rigid, limited-scope point solutions or custom solutions built in-house, which demand immense expertise and investment.



That is…until now.



Enter Lyric: Lyric is an enterprise AI platform built specifically for supply chains, offering the best of both worlds:

 - Out-of-the-box AI solutions for optimizing networks, allocating inventory, scheduling routes, planning fulfillment capacity, promising orders, propagating demand, building predictions, analyzing scenarios, and more, plus

 - A platform-first approach that empowers both business and technical users with end-to-end product composability, leveraging no-code tools, their own code, or even forking our code to build and refine supply chain decision intelligence

With Lyric, enterprises no longer have to choose between flexibility and speed, they get both.



The Mission: We’re building a new era in supply chain with the team best equipped to lead it. With over 20 years at the intersection of supply chain and algorithms, we developed a deep conviction that global supply chains needed something like Lyric. Since our inception in December 2021, that conviction has been validated time and time again.



Today, a growing number of Fortune 500 companies, including Smurfit WestRock, Estée Lauder, Coca-Cola, and more, are innovating on their own terms with Lyric. We can’t wait to see what our customers, both current and future, are empowered to build with us next. Come build with us!

About the Role

We’re hiring Growth Strategists: high-agency, client-facing professionals who will be the tip of the spear for Lyric’s go-to-market approach. This is not a traditional business development role. You’ll be embedded with senior sales leaders and cross-functional teams, opening new accounts while building real expertise in the enterprise GTM motion.

This role is designed for driven early-career professionals or post-MBA candidates looking to break into a high-growth startup. You don’t need enterprise AI or supply chain experience, just the curiosity and work ethic to learn it fast.



What You’ll Do

 - Identify, research, and develop new account opportunities across target verticals

 - Conduct outbound prospecting through multi-channel campaigns (email, phone, LinkedIn, events)

 - Shadow senior AEs and GTM leaders on client meetings and demos to build domain fluency and selling skills

 - Help prepare demo environments, RFP responses, and opportunity materials for active deals

 - Join opportunity teams to support key pursuit strategy and client engagement

 - Contribute to broader GTM activities: market research, competitive intelligence, event support, and content collaboration

 - Develop a point of view on target accounts and industries that sharpens the team’s overall strategy

Who You Are

 - Early career (2+ years of professional experience) or post-MBA candidate looking to pivot into enterprise AI technology

 - Exceptional communicator; polished, articulate, and comfortable in client-facing settings

 - High agency: you take ownership, follow through, and don’t wait to be told what to do

 - Intellectually curious with a hunger to learn about supply chain, technology, and enterprise sales

 - Strong researcher; you can quickly synthesize complex information and present it clearly

 - Resourceful with tools and technology. You're the kind of person who finds the right shortcut or AI-powered workflow to 10x your output before anyone asks you to

 - Collaborative and team-oriented; comfortable in a fast-moving, ambiguous environment
   
   

Why This Role

 - Career path into AE, pre-sales, solution design, revenue o

What You’ll Do

At Lyric, we’re building a modern decision intelligence platform that helps businesses move faster, think smarter, and act confidently. But great software is only great if people know how to use it. That’s where you come in.
As a Technical Writer, you’ll turn complex product functionality into content that’s clear and easy to use. From user guides to release notes, in-product help to walkthrough videos, you'll create documentation that supports our users and reflects our brand voice.
This is more than just a writing job — it’s a chance to collaborate with cross-functional teams, experiment with new tools (especially AI), and continuously improve how we deliver content.


What You'll Be Writing (and Doing)
User Guides – Create and maintain clear, concise guides that help users understand and navigate Lyric Studio and related features.
Release Notes – Draft structured, scannable release notes that highlight what’s new, improved, or fixed — and why it matters.
In-Product Help – Contribute to tooltips, guided tours, and inline help to support users directly in the product.
UX Content Reviews – Work with product and design teams to review in-app copy, such as labels, messages, and tooltips, to ensure clarity and consistency.
Visual & Multimedia Content – Help script product walkthrough videos, create annotated visuals, and support other visual documentation efforts.

Content Improvement Loops – Use internal feedback, user queries, and analytics to iterate and improve documentation over time.
AI Tool Exploration – Continuously explore and test AI and productivity tools that can help us write better and faster. We encourage ongoing experimentation.
Contribute Ideas – Share ideas to improve how we work, what we write, and how we deliver documentation to users.
Deliver on Timelines – Prioritize tasks and manage your time effectively to meet documentation deadlines in sync with product releases.



Tools You Might Use
Authoring & Collaboration – GitBook, Markdown, GitHub, Confluence.
In-Product Help – Whatfix.
Design & UX – Figma.

Visuals & Videos – SnagIt, Synthesia.
Don’t worry if you’re new to some of these — we’ll help you get u

source_jobs.description:

Powerful algorithms and AI can address these problems, yet most organizations struggle to leverage supply chain AI at scale. That is…until now.



Enter Lyric: Lyric is an enterprise AI platform built specifically for supply chains, offering the best of both worlds:

 - Out-of-the-box AI solutions for optimizing networks, allocating inventory, scheduling routes, planning fulfillment capacity, promising orders, propagating demand, building predictions, analyzing scenarios, and more, plus

 - A platform-first approach that empowers both business and technical users with end-to-end product composability, leveraging no-code tools, their own code, or even forking our code to build and refine supply chain decision intelligence

With Lyric, enterprises no longer have to choose between flexibility and speed, they get both.



The Mission: We’re building a new era in supply chain with the team best equipped to lead it. With over 20 years at the intersection of supply chain and algorithms, we developed a deep conviction that global supply chains needed something like Lyric. Since our inception in December 2021, that conviction has been validated time and time again.



Today, a growing number of Fortune 500 companies, including Smurfit WestRock, Estée Lauder, Coca-Cola, and more, are innovating on their own terms with Lyric. We can’t wait to see what our customers, both current and future, are empowered to build with us next. Come build with us!


WHO WE ARE

We're problem solvers and optimists who are genuinely, unapologetically obsessed with math for supply chains. We believe that the best decisions are powered by algorithms — and that the world's most complex supply chains deserve better than the legacy tools they've been stuck with for decades. We move fast. Like, #mach10 fast. If you thrive in an environment where ambiguity is a feature, speed is a value, and the work actually matters — you'll fit right in.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The era of siloed supply chains is over. Planning cycles that once took weeks now run in minutes. Decisions that once required armies of analysts can be augmented — or automated — with the right intelligence. Lyric is building the platform that makes this possible: an AI-native decision intelligence platform designed to dismantle functional silos and replace them with composable, connected solutions that let global enterprises move at the speed of thought.



Think of this less as a traditional implementation consultant role and more in the spirit of a Forward Deployed Supply Chain Engineer — embedded with customers, building in the field, and deploying decision intelligence directly into the heart of their operations. You're the bridge between Lyric's platform capabilities and mission-critical execution — part solution architect, part AI practitioner, part hands-on engineer. You're not handing over a configured system and walking away. You're working alongside customer teams until the right decisions are happening faster, smarter, and with less friction than before.



This is a senior, customer-facing role. You'll translate real-world supply chain complexity — network design trade-offs, demand volatility, inventory risk, fulfillment constraints — into composable, AI-native solutions on Lyric's platform, owning that translation end to end from the first discovery session to go-live and beyond. You won't be recommending strategies from a slide deck. You'll be turning "what if" into "here's how" — using Lyric's composable architecture to build everything from probabilistic simulation models to ML-powered planning workflows.



Our delivery teams are lean

About the role

As an Industry Solutions Principal at Lyric, you will join us at a pivotal moment in our growth journey. Reporting directly to the Head of Revenue, you'll partner with our GTM team to drive technical wins and establish Lyric as the vendor of choice in competitive sales cycles. This role combines deep supply chain planning expertise with hands-on technical execution. You'll partner with Sales, Product, and customers to design and articulate how Lyric's platform solves complex operational problems, ensuring technical clarity, solution feasibility, and alignment to pre-agreed value drivers. The ideal candidate will thrive at the intersection of technical understanding of modern data pipelines and AI/ML capabilities, business and solution understanding of supply chain processes, and commercial experience supporting enterprise sales cycles.




What you’ll do

 - Drive Technical Wins: Lead solution design, demos, and POVs with prospects and customers to secure technical wins and wow our customers in competitive cycles.

 - Create Industry Playbooks: Develop repeatable GTM plays for your 1-2 industry verticals, including personas, discovery questions, competitive positioning, and value propositions.

 - Shape Messaging: Partner with Product and Marketing to translate technical capabilities into business outcomes and industry-specific points of view.

 - Enable GTM Teams: Equip Sales, Solutions, and Customer Success with tools, playbooks, training and for high-impact conversations.

 - Engage Executives: Participate in executive meetings and key pursuits to validate solution fit and shape deal strategy.

 - Represent Lyric: Speak at industry events and customer forums as a thought leader.




Who you are

 - Experienced Solutions Expert: 12-15+ years in Consulting, Supply Chain Operations, or Go-to-Market Strategy roles in supply chain technology or supply chain technology consulting companies.

 - Industry Fluent: Deep understanding of at least two core verticals relevant to Lyric’s target markets.

 - Technical Proficiency: Strong understanding of modern data pipelines, ETL processes, analytics, and emerging AI/ML capabilities

source_jobs.description:

About the role

Lyric is a platform for building AI-powered supply chain products. The Product Training team is on a mission to enable 1 million people to do supply chain engineering, and we need builders and educators to help us get there.

This is an entry-level role for recent graduates with strong technical foundations and a passion for making hard things understandable.



What You'll Do

 - Develop self-paced learning content that teaches math-first thinking and supply chain engineering workflows, including video courses, written modules, exercises, and toy datasets

 - Build technical training materials covering mathematical modeling, optimization, data science, supply chain planning, and data visualization

 - Contribute to repeatable training modules, playbooks, and structured product curricula

 - Shadow and support in-person and virtual customer workshops

 - Help facilitate technical modeling sessions alongside senior team members

 - Take complex concepts like optimization, forecasting, and AI agents and make them intuitive and actionable for customers



What We're Looking For

 - Strong technical foundation in mathematical modeling, optimization, data science, industrial engineering, operations research, statistics, or computer science

 - Comfort reasoning about algorithms and translating technical concepts into clear explanations

 - Strong storytelling and teaching instincts: you can make hard things understandable

 - Bonus: experience creating educational content, writing technical docs, or presenting to non-expert audiences



Location & Travel

Chicago-based or open to relocation, hybrid (2–3 days/week in the Loop). Domestic and international travel to support customer workshops is expected to reach 25–50% over time.



Compensation

The annual base compensation range for this position is $80,000 and $90,000. Base cash will be determined based on years of experience with a 20% variable compensation that is earned based on individual & business performance. You will also have the opportunity to receive equity in Lyric.

 

Even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications, we recommend applying to the role!

Today, a growing number of Fortune 500 companies, including Smurfit WestRock, Estée Lauder, Coca-Cola, and more, are innovating on their own terms with Lyric. We can’t wait to see what our customers, both current and future, are empowered to build with us next. Come build with us!


POSITION OVERVIEW

Lyric is looking for an ML (Data) Platform Engineer to help scale our AutoML platform — a system deeply intertwined with data management, feature engineering, and time series forecasting at scale. You’ll play a foundational role in building scalable, composable data infrastructure and pipelines that support both ML and analytics use cases.

This is not a vanilla ETL role — you’ll work on building data platforms that underpin automated model building, experimentation, and data lineage systems with high SLA requirements. If you thrive at the intersection of ML, data infrastructure, and platform thinking, this is a high-impact opportunity.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and scale data management systems that power our AutoML and forecasting platforms

 - Own and evolve the feature store and feature engineering workflows

 - Implement robust data SLAs and lineage systems across time series data pipelines

 - Collaborate with ML engineers, infra, and product teams to ensure scalable and user-aware platform design

 - Drive architectural decisions around data distribution, versioning, and composability

 - Participate in the design of reusable systems for varied supply chain problems
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

Must-Have:

 - Strong experience working with large-scale data systems (Big Data, distributed pipelines)

 - Hands-on experience with ETL pipelines, data lineage, and data reliability tooling

 - Proven experience in ML feature engineering and/or building feature stores

 - Exposure to time series data, forecasting pipelines, or AutoML workflows

 - Strong problem-solving and design thinking ability — can break down ambiguous platform problems
   

Good-to-Have:

 - Familiarity with modern data infra (e.g., Apache Iceberg, ClickHouse, Data Lakes)

 - Product thinking — can anticipate how users will interact with the system and build accordingly

 - Experience building composable, user-extensible systems

 - Prior exposure to AutoML frameworks (e.g., SageMaker, VertexAI) or internal ML platforms

About the role

As Technical Presales Specialist at Lyric, you will join us at a pivotal moment in our growth journey. Working closely with our Sales and Product teams, you'll be responsible for architecting and demonstrating technical solutions that showcase the power of our enterprise AI platform. This role combines deep technical expertise with consultative selling—you'll craft compelling technical narratives and demonstrations while supporting our sales team in complex enterprise deals. The ideal candidate will thrive in translating complex supply chain challenges into tangible AI-powered solutions, bringing technical credibility and domain expertise to every customer interaction.

 

What you’ll do

 - Lead technical discovery sessions and deliver compelling product demonstrations that showcase Lyric's platform capabilities and business value

 - Partner with Account Executives to develop technical win strategies and overcome objections throughout the sales cycle

 - Create technical sales enablement materials and provide architectural guidance during customer implementations

 - Stay current with supply chain technology trends to effectively position Lyric's technical differentiators

 

Who you are

 - 5+ years of technical presales or solutions engineering experience in enterprise software, with strong background in either supply chain software or AI/ML platforms

 - Deep understanding of supply chain processes and technologies, with ability to translate complex technical concepts into business value

 - Strong presentation and demo skills with experience speaking to both technical and executive-level audiences

 - Excellent project management skills with ability to handle multiple complex sales cycles simultaneously

 

Even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications, we recommend applying to the role!

 

Location: Remote - DACH: Germany, Austria, Switzerland. Some travel is required.

 

Compensation: We provide a competitive mix of salary, commissions, local benefits, and equity, designed to reward both your impact and growth.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer -  Notes
source_jobs.description:
About the Company

Why We Built Lyric: Supply chains are more critical and complex than ever. Every day, large enterprises navigate trillions of possible decisions that could impact the bottom line. Powerful algorithms and AI can address these problems, yet most organizations struggle to leverage supply chain AI at scale. The current SCM technologies are either rigid, limited-scope point solutions or custom solutions built in-house, which demand immense expertise and investment.



That is…until now.



Enter Lyric: Lyric is an enterprise AI platform built specifically for supply chains, offering the best of both worlds:

 - Out-of-the-box AI solutions for optimizing networks, allocating inventory, scheduling routes, planning fulfillment capacity, promising orders, propagating demand, building predictions, analyzing scenarios, and more, plus

 - A platform-first approach that empowers both business and technical users with end-to-end product composability, leveraging no-code tools, their own code, or even forking our code to build and refine supply chain decision intelligence

With Lyric, enterprises no longer have to choose between flexibility and speed, they get both.



The Mission: We’re building a new era in supply chain with the team best equipped to lead it. With over 20 years at the intersection of supply chain and algorithms, we developed a deep conviction that global supply chains needed something like Lyric. Since our inception in December 2021, that conviction has been validated time and time again.



Today, a growing number of Fortune 500 companies, including Smurfit WestRock, Estée Lauder, Coca-Cola, and more, are innovating on their own terms with Lyric. We can’t wait to see what our customers, both current and future, are empowered to build with us next. Come build with us!


ABOUT THE ROLE

At Lyric, our mission is to empower supply chain teams with intelligence and automation. A critical part of this vision is our Notes Execution Platform, which enables users to author, run, and share external and internal code securely and seamlessly within Lyric’s environment.

We are looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to lead the design and development of this platform — building a secure, scalable, and developer-friendly execution engine that integrates deeply with Lyric’s data and ML workflows.

You will design APIs and services to orchestrate code execution, manage resources, isolate environments, and provide observability into execution lifecycles. You’ll also tackle hard engineering problems around sandboxing, dependency management, concurrency, and horizontal scalability.

This is a high-impact, foundational role at the intersection of developer productivity, data engineering, and platform infrastructure.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design, build, and scale the Notes Execution Platform at Lyric — enabling seamless execution of internal and external code in a secure, isolated, and observable way.

 - Architect sandboxed environments to run untrusted or user-supplied code with appropriate resource quotas and security controls.

 - Develop backend services and APIs to manage job lifecycles: scheduling, monitoring, cancellation, logging, and reporting.

 - Ensure the system is resilient and scales horizontally to meet growing demand.

 - Integrate the Notes platform with Lyric’s data platform and ML workflows, making it a first-class citizen in our ecosystem.

 - Collaborate with product managers, data scientists, and engineers to understand use cases and deliver intuitive developer experiences.

 - Drive technical excellence: establish best practices in code execution safety, performance tuning, and observability.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of backend engineering experience, ideally building distributed systems or platform infrastructure.

 - Deep expertise in at least one backend language (e.g., Python, Go, Java, or similar) and experience designing RESTful and/or gRPC APIs.

 - Experience with containerization

What You’ll Do


STRATEGIC PLANNING & FINANCE LEADERSHIP

 - Own and maintain Lyric's integrated operating model and 3-statement financial model

 - Lead annual planning, forecasting, budgeting, and long-range planning processes

 - Build driver-based forecasts across ARR, services revenue, headcount, utilization, margins, and cash flow

 - Develop scenario analyses around growth investments, hiring plans, deal structures, pricing, and services attach rates

BUSINESS PARTNERSHIP

 - Serve as a strategic finance partner to leaders across the business

 - Drive visibility into business performance through reporting, KPI development, and financial analysis

source_jobs.title: Technical Presales Specialist (UK)
source_jobs.description:
About the Company

Why We Built Lyric: Supply chains are more critical and complex than ever. Every day, large enterprises navigate trillions of possible decisions that could impact the bottom line. Powerful algorithms and AI can address these problems, yet most organizations struggle to leverage supply chain AI at scale. The current SCM technologies are either rigid, limited-scope point solutions or custom solutions built in-house, which demand immense expertise and investment.



That is…until now.



Enter Lyric: Lyric is an enterprise AI platform built specifically for supply chains, offering the best of both worlds:

 - Out-of-the-box AI solutions for optimizing networks, allocating inventory, scheduling routes, planning fulfillment capacity, promising orders, propagating demand, building predictions, analyzing scenarios, and more, plus

 - A platform-first approach that empowers both business and technical users with end-to-end product composability, leveraging no-code tools, their own code, or even forking our code to build and refine supply chain decision intelligence

With Lyric, enterprises no longer have to choose between flexibility and speed, they get both.



The Mission: We’re building a new era in supply chain with the team best equipped to lead it. With over 20 years at the intersection of supply chain and algorithms, we developed a deep conviction that global supply chains needed something like Lyric. Since our inception in December 2021, that conviction has been validated time and time again.



Today, a growing number of Fortune 500 companies, including Smurfit WestRock, Estée Lauder, Coca-Cola, and more, are innovating on their own terms with Lyric. We can’t wait to see what our customers, both current and future, are empowered to build with us next. Come build with us!



About the role

As Technical Presales Specialist at Lyric, you will join us at a pivotal moment in our growth journey. Working closely with our Sales and Product teams, you'll be responsible for architecting and demonstrating technical solutions that showcase the power of our enterprise AI platform. This role combines deep technical expertise with consultative selling—you'll craft compelling technical narratives and demonstrations while supporting our sales team in complex enterprise deals. The ideal candidate will thrive in translating complex supply chain challenges into tangible AI-powered solutions, bringing technical credibility and domain expertise to every customer interaction.



What you’ll do

 - Lead technical discovery sessions and deliver compelling product demonstrations that showcase Lyric's platform capabilities and business value

 - Partner with Account Executives to develop technical win strategies and overcome objections throughout the sales cycle

 - Create technical sales enablement materials and provide architectural guidance during customer implementations

 - Stay current with supply chain technology trends to effectively position Lyric's technical differentiators




Who you are

 - 5+ years of technical presales or solutions engineering experience in enterprise software, with strong background in either supply chain software or AI/ML platforms

 - Deep understanding of supply chain processes and technologies, with ability to translate complex technical concepts into business value

 - Strong presentation and demo skills with experience speaking to both technical and executive-level audiences

 - Excellent project management skills with ability to handle multiple complex sales cycles simultaneously



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications, we recommend applying to the role!

 

Location: Remote - UK. Some travel required.

 

Compensation: We provide a competitive mix of salary, commissions, local benefits, and equity, designed to reward both your impact and growth.

We're looking for a Senior Product Designer to own how our AI-first supply chain platform looks, feels, and tells stories through data.

Lyric is a decision-intelligence platform for the supply chain. Teams use Lyric Studio to solve genuinely hard problems — inventory planning, network design, order forensics, transportation optimization — with ML and mathematical optimization under the hood. Your job is to make all of that complexity feel simple, coherent, and powerful.

This is a high-ownership seat on a deliberately lean, growing design team. You'll drive work end-to-end and bring a point of view — not wait to be handed tasks or asked to pick between three versions.




🧩 WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the look and feel. Drive the platform's visual experience, with a primary focus on dashboards, widgets, and data visualizations — how they work, how they're configured, and how they communicate.

 - Tell stories with data. Turn complex, data-heavy workflows into clear stories. Know when a metric wants a donut, a bar, or both — and always tie the visual back to the business outcome it serves.

 - Drive design end-to-end. From uncovering the real problem and asking the right questions, to a defensible direction, to a polished, shipped result.

 - Partner across the company. Work with product, engineering, and customer success to stand up sharp, compelling experiences — including custom demos when the moment calls for it.

 - Reason about outcomes. Think in terms of conversion, information architecture, and business value — on top of making things genuinely beautiful.
   
   


🧠 WHAT YOU BRING

Must-Haves

 - A strong visual instinct. Aesthetics matter a lot here — you can make something look genuinely good, and you trust your eye.

 - A data-storytelling sense. You think about what a chart should say, not just what it should show.

 - High agency. You research, decide, and defend a direction — you don't offer a menu for someone else to pick from.

 - End-to-end ownership. You treat scope as yours to figure out, comfortable taking a problem from zero to one and from one to ten.
